channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In the shadow of the Olympics, migrants search for a welcome in Milan
item.description: As Italy cracks down on migration, Milan takes a different path — offering shelter and integration to asylum seekers even as the central government tightens borders and funds deterrence abroad.
item.content:encoded:
As Italy cracks down on migration, Milan takes a different path — offering shelter and integration to asylum seekers even as the central government tightens borders and funds deterrence abroad.

(Image credit: Valerio Muscella for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Trump to raise global tariffs. And, most say the state of the union is weak, poll says
item.description: President Trump says he is raising global tariffs to 15%. And ahead of the president's address tomorrow, most Americans say the state of the union is not strong, according to an NPR poll.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump says he is raising global tariffs to 15%. And ahead of the president's address tomorrow, most Americans say the state of the union is not strong, according to an NPR poll.

(Image credit: Al Drago)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: U.S. has a quarter fewer immigration judges than it did a year ago. Here's why
item.description: The continued drain of personnel from the already strained immigration court system has contributed to depleted staff morale, mounting case backlogs — and floundering due process.
item.content:encoded:
The continued drain of personnel from the already strained immigration court system has contributed to depleted staff morale, mounting case backlogs — and floundering due process.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Anusha Mathur

item.title: The owners want to close this Colorado coal plant. The Trump administration says no
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered several coal plants to keep operating past their planned retirement, part of a larger effort to boost the coal industry. Two Colorado utilities are pushing back.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has ordered several coal plants to keep operating past their planned retirement, part of a larger effort to boost the coal industry. Two Colorado utilities are pushing back.

(Image credit: Hart Van Denburg)
item.dc:creator: Sam Brasch

item.title: Influencers are promoting peptides for better health. What does the science say?
item.description: The latest wellness craze involves injecting these molecules for athletic performance, longevity and more. Scientists say the research isn't keeping pace with the health claims.
item.content:encoded: The latest wellness craze involves injecting these molecules for athletic performance, longevity and more. Scientists say the research isn't keeping pace with the health claims.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden

item.title: Poll: Most say the state of the union is not strong and the U.S. is worse off
item.description: Ahead of the State of the Union address on Tuesday, evidence continues to mount that President Trump is facing political headwinds.
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of the State of the Union address on Tuesday, evidence continues to mount that President Trump is facing political headwinds.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The political implications of the SCOTUS ruling on Trump's tariffs, China urges Washington to lift tariffs after SCOTUS decision, new poll reveals what Americans think of the state of the union.
item.content:encoded: The political implications of the SCOTUS ruling on Trump's tariffs, China urges Washington to lift tariffs after SCOTUS decision, new poll reveals what Americans think of the state of the union.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: U.S. boosts gold medal tally at Winter Games, led by athletes from handful of states
item.description: The U.S. is a winter sport powerhouse, second only to Norway. The wins at the Milan Cortina Games come disproportionately from athletes who emerged in a handful of states from Alaska to Vermont.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. is a winter sport powerhouse, second only to Norway. The wins at the Milan Cortina Games come disproportionately from athletes who emerged in a handful of states from Alaska to Vermont.

(Image credit: Robert F. Bukaty)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Mexico fears more violence after army kills leader of powerful Jalisco cartel
item.description: School was canceled in several Mexican states and local and foreign governments alike warned their citizens to stay inside following the army's killing of the leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, "El Mencho," and the violence it spurred
item.content:encoded:
School was canceled in several Mexican states and local and foreign governments alike warned their citizens to stay inside following the army's killing of the leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, "El Mencho," and the violence it spurred

(Image credit: Armando Solis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Newly discovered dinosaur species was a fish-eater with a huge horn
item.description: The semi-aquatic dinosaur, Spinosaurus mirabilis, was discovered by an international team of scientists working in Niger.
item.content:encoded:
The semi-aquatic dinosaur, Spinosaurus mirabilis, was discovered by an international team of scientists working in Niger.

(Image credit: Matthew Irving)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher
